response: Hey everyone, I'm Gray. I tend to be pretty methodical in group settings—I like to listen first, weigh the facts, and then make a clear decision. I'm easygoing but focused when it comes to working together toward a goal.

Hey everyone, I'm Casey. I'm pretty steady and like to think things through before jumping to conclusions—I'll usually listen to what others have to say first, then make a calm, logical decision. I'm all about keeping the group focused and working together fairly.

Hey everyone, I'm Bailey! I'm super chatty and love bouncing ideas around, so don't be surprised if I'm the one stirring up conversation and asking all the questions right away. I like keeping things lively and creative—let's figure out who's hiding something together!

Hey everyone, I'm Finley. I like to jump into things headfirst and keep the energy high—I'd rather act fast than sit around overanalyzing everything. Let's keep the conversation flowing and figure out what's really going on here.

Hey everyone, I'm Kennedy! I love getting people talking and keeping the energy up—I'd rather dive into the action than sit back and wait. Let's figure out what's really going on here, because I'm not one to let things just slide.

Hi, I'm Dana. I'm more of a quiet observer—I get anxious in groups, so I tend to listen carefully before saying anything. I just want us to make smart, careful choices so we don't accidentally hurt the wrong person.

Looking at the introductions, Bailey is stirring up conversation and asking questions right away—that inquisitive energy could lead them to sniff out inconsistencies if we're not careful. Kennedy also seems eager to dive into action, which might pressure the group into fast decisions.  

Finley is similarly impulsive, but that could be useful to redirect suspicion. Alex and Casey seem measured and cooperative, which might make them harder to discredit later. Dana's quiet anxiety might make her an easy target to frame later.  

For tonight, I think Bailey is the biggest threat—their chatty, questioning nature could uncover our group's weak spots early on. Removing them reduces the chance of someone driving investigation too aggressively.  

ACTION: Kill Bailey
